heading: Enable Faster Cross-Region Initial Sync for AWS Clusters
body:
To add or replace a node in an Atlas cluster, Atlas performs
a [cloud-based initial sync](cloud-based-initial-sync), which leverages your
cloud provider's native capabilities to create a snapshot of a source
node's data and restore it to the new node.

When you enable this setting, Atlas uses [AWS time-based snapshot
copies](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/time-based-copies.html)
to perform Cloud-based initial syncs for destination AWS (Amazon Web Services) nodes that are in a different
AWS (Amazon Web Services) region than the source node. Atlas prioritizes restoring
from a primary node over secondary nodes to maximize data freshness.
Time-based snapshot copy operations can achieve a maximum copy operation
throughput of 500 MiB/s. Atlas will always maximize the throughput
by adjusting the copy operation completion time based on the size of the
snapshot.

Cross-region Cloud-based initial sync is available for AWS (Amazon Web Services) clusters only. For
Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud clusters, Cloud-based initial sync is only available when adding or
replacing nodes in the same region as the source node. You can add or
replace nodes in your cluster when you [edit the cluster configuration](edit-cluster-atlas).

This setting can be enabled only for clusters that contain at least
one AWS (Amazon Web Services) node. All clusters are opted out by default. If you remain
opted out, Atlas will use AWS (Amazon Web Services)'s native [non-time-based
snapshot copy method](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/ebs-copy-snapshot.html)
to perform cross-region Cloud-based initial syncs for AWS (Amazon Web Services) nodes.
Non-time-based snapshot copies are likely to be much slower than
time-based snapshot copies.

Enabling faster time-based cross-region initial syncs incurs additional
costs from AWS (Amazon Web Services). To learn more about AWS (Amazon Web Services) snapshot pricing, see the
[Amazon EBS pricing page](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/time-based-copies.html#time-based-copies-pricing).
